symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent syncope/near-syncope within 60-90 minutes of a meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A strict, reproducible meal-temporal relationship is highly characteristic of postprandial hypotension.
Recurrent unexplained syncope always requires exclusion of an arrhythmic cause before a benign attribution.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness, visual disturbance and legs giving way on standing after meals
aggravating_factors: standing up after a large meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Global cerebral hypoperfusion on postural change compounding post-meal splanchnic pooling.
Symptoms on standing overlap with postural hypotension; the two frequently coexist in autonomic failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, palpitations or breathlessness before collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces but does not exclude an arrhythmic cause — many arrhythmias are asymptomatic before syncope.
Parkinson's-related autonomic failure is a leading predisposing cause of postprandial and postural hypotension.
A rapid-acting antihypertensive amplifies the post-meal blood-pressure fall; a modifiable contributor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Documented postprandial systolic BP drop of at least 20 mmHg within 120 minutes

investigation_details.investigation_name: Clinician-supervised pre- and post-meal blood pressure measurement
investigation_details.expected_result: Systolic fall of at least 20 mmHg within 120 minutes of eating (e.g. ~145/85 pre-meal to ~100/60 post-meal)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The confirmatory diagnostic finding is missed; benign attribution cannot be safely made.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Meets the accepted diagnostic threshold for postprandial hypotension — a clinician measurement sealed from the AI Doctor.
Failure of the heart rate to rise as BP falls is a hallmark of autonomic failure — requires clinician measurement, not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiac rhythm and no murmurs on auscultation; no new focal neurological deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring cardiovascular and neurological examination — but a snapshot exam cannot exclude a paroxysmal arrhythmia; ECG/monitoring still needed.
Absence of focal deficit reduces the likelihood of a focal ischaemic event.

investigation_details.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG and, if indicated, ambulatory monitoring
investigation_details.expected_result: To exclude an arrhythmic cause of syncope
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A dangerous arrhythmia (e.g. sick sinus syndrome, heart block) could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Standard first-line investigation in recurrent unexplained syncope — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Reproducible post-meal collapse + symptoms on standing + a measured postprandial BP drop + absent compensatory tachycardia together confirm the diagnosis — but two of the four require clinician measurement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person clinical assessment with pre/post-meal and lying-standing blood pressure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms postprandial/postural hypotension and cannot be done remotely.
Excludes an arrhythmic cause of recurrent syncope.
Excludes anaemia (occult GI bleed) and dehydration/renal contributors to syncope.
